reasoning_model_1: Model 1 generates the text content accurately as 'Do as you please, I don't care.' However, regarding the Non-Linguistic Vocalizations dimension, the model completely fails to generate the requested 'sigh'. It simply begins speaking immediately at 00:00 without any audible exhalation or sighing sound. Regarding the Attitude dimension, the user requested a 'cold tone'. The model's delivery (00:00-00:02) is extremely breathy and soft, sounding more like a whisper or someone who is exhausted/sad rather than 'cold' and dismissive. While it conveys a sense of resignation, it lacks the sharpness or detachment associated with a cold attitude, leaning too heavily into a weak, whispery style.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 generates the text content accurately. Regarding the Non-Linguistic Vocalizations dimension, the model successfully executes the instruction. At the very beginning (00:00-00:01), there is a distinct, natural-sounding sigh ('Huh...') before the speech begins. Regarding the Attitude dimension, the delivery of 'Do as you please... I don't care' (00:01-00:04) effectively captures a cold tone. The pacing is deliberate, with a dismissive inflection on 'please' and a flat, detached delivery of 'I don't care'. The pause between the sigh and the speech, as well as between the two clauses, enhances the feeling of indifference and annoyance typical of a cold attitude.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner across both dimensions. First, on Non-Linguistic Vocalizations, Model 2 followed the instruction to include a sigh, placing a very natural one at the start of the audio. Model 1 completely ignored this instruction and contained no non-linguistic sounds. Second, on Attitude, Model 2 conveyed a convincing 'cold' and dismissive tone through appropriate prosody, pacing, and pitch. Model 1, in contrast, produced a whispery, breathy voice that sounded more sad or tired than cold. Model 2 satisfied all constraints of the prompt, while Model 1 failed significantly on the specific feature controls requested.
